edibility: Generally considered inedible due to its tough, leathery texture. Some mycology guides note that very young specimens may be edible, but this species is rarely sought for culinary purposes.
taste: Mild but tough and leathery in texture, making it unsuitable for consumption.
search: Found in spring through early summer on dead hardwood twigs and branches. Identification is aided by its distinctive large diamond-shaped pores. Look-alikes include other polypores, but the hexagonal pore structure is characteristic.
